sourceMovie.title: Death Race 2
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Death Race 2 (2010) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: What Happened to Monday
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Death Race 2 clicked for you, What Happened to Monday (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Death Race 2?
faq.answer: Try What Happened to Monday, Mad Max, The Running Man—each shares DNA with Death Race 2 while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Death Race 2?
faq.answer: Death Race 2 (2010) is best described as Action, Thriller, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Death Race 2 part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Death Race (2008) Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
